reasoning:
Valero just reported its Q1 2025 earnings on April 24, missing expectations fundamentally with a $595m net loss and a $1B impairment charge, due to squeezing refining margins and overcapacity. Despite this genuinely horrible news, the stock popped by over 6% (from ~$104 up to $110.6) on the earnings drop, likely serving as a relief rally because the market had expected even worse or was heavily shorted, or because management signaled Q2 improvements in gasoline cracks as summer driving season approaches.

Because of this surprise volatility counter to fundamentals, short-term momentum is hard to predict. 
For 2025-05-04 (1-week horizon), typical post-earnings relief rallies often give back some gains as reality sets in and institutional rebalancing occurs. We project a slight bearishness initially (49%).
For 2025-05-27 (1-month horizon), momentum stabilizes. Historically, refining margins improve leading into the summer; however, this sits dangerously close to an expected late-May ex-dividend date. Since the ex-dividend hasn't occurred yet, the price doesn't suffer the drop. We estimate 50%.
For 2025-07-26 (3-month horizon), we will have seen one ex-dividend structural price drop (~$1.13). However, 3 months of an expected equity risk premium roughly offsets the dividend drop. We raise probability to 51%. 
For 2025-10-24 (6-month horizon), two dividend distributions will have occurred (~$2.26 total drag). Over 6 months, stock indices and broader market beta generally appreciate enough to cover dividend slips. Uncertainty rises with the horizon, pulling estimates closer to 0.5, but the underlying drift nudges this to 52%.
